InstanceId.description: The EC2 instance ID associated with the event, if applicable.

ResourceType.description: The type of resource associated with the event. Valid values are Cluster, InstanceGroup, or Instance.

EventDetails.description: Additional details about the event, including event-specific metadata.

EventMetadata.description: Metadata specific to the event, which may include information about the cluster, instance group, or instance involved.

FailureMessage.description: An error message describing why the cluster level operation (such as creating, updating, or deleting) failed.

EksRoleAccessEntries.description: A list of Amazon EKS IAM role ARNs associated with the cluster. This is created by HyperPod on your behalf and only applies for EKS orchestrated clusters.

SlrAccessEntry.description: The Service-Linked Role (SLR) associated with the cluster. This is created by HyperPod on your behalf and only applies for EKS orchestrated clusters.

FailureMessage.description: An error message describing why the instance group level operation (such as creating, scaling, or deleting) failed.

AvailabilityZoneId.description: The ID of the Availability Zone where the instance group is located.

CapacityReservation.description: Information about the Capacity Reservation used by the instance group.

SubnetId.description: The ID of the subnet where the instance group is located.

SecurityGroupIds.description: A list of security group IDs associated with the instance group.

AmiOverride.description: If you use a custom Amazon Machine Image (AMI) for the instance group, this field shows the ID of the custom AMI.

InstanceGroupScaling.description: Metadata related to instance group scaling events.

InstanceCount.description: The current number of instances in the group.

TargetCount.description: The desired number of instances for the group after scaling.

MinCount.description: Minimum instance count of the instance group.

FailureMessage.description: An error message describing why the scaling operation failed, if applicable.

CustomerEni.description: The ID of the customer-managed Elastic Network Interface (ENI) associated with the instance.

AdditionalEnis.description: Information about additional Elastic Network Interfaces (ENIs) associated with the instance.

CapacityReservation.description: Information about the Capacity Reservation used by the instance.

FailureMessage.description: An error message describing why the instance creation or update failed, if applicable.

LcsExecutionState.description: The execution state of the Lifecycle Script (LCS) for the instance.

NodeLogicalId.description: The unique logical identifier of the node within the cluster. The ID used here is the same object as in the BatchAddClusterNodes API.

NodeDetails.description: The details of the SageMaker HyperPod cluster node.

InstanceGroupName.description: The instance group name in which the instance is.

NodeLogicalId.description: A unique identifier for the node that persists throughout its lifecycle, from provisioning request to termination. This identifier can be used to track the node even before it has an assigned InstanceId.

Status.description: The status of an instance in a SageMaker HyperPod cluster.

Message.description: The message from an instance in a SageMaker HyperPod cluster.

InstanceType.description: The type of the instance.

LaunchTime.description: The time when the instance is launched.

LastSoftwareUpdateTime.description: The time when the cluster was last updated.

LifeCycleConfig.description: The LifeCycle configuration applied to the instance.

SourceS3Uri.description:
An Amazon S3 bucket path where your lifecycle scripts are stored.

Make sure that the S3 bucket path starts with s3://sagemaker-. The IAM role for SageMaker HyperPod has the managed AmazonSageMakerClusterInstanceRolePolicy attached, which allows access to S3 buckets with the specific prefix sagemaker-.

OnCreate.description: The file name of the entrypoint script of lifecycle scripts under SourceS3Uri. This entrypoint script runs during cluster creation.

OverrideVpcConfig.description: The customized Amazon VPC configuration at the instance group level that overrides the default Amazon VPC configuration of the SageMaker HyperPod cluster.

SecurityGroupIds.description: The VPC security group IDs, in the form sg-xxxxxxxx. Specify the security groups for the VPC that is specified in the Subnets field.

Subnets.description: The ID of the subnets in the VPC to which you want to connect your training job or model. For information about the availability of specific instance types, see Supported Instance Types and Availability Zones.

ThreadsPerCore.description: The number of threads per CPU core you specified under CreateCluster.

InstanceStorageConfigs.description: The configurations of additional storage specified to the instance group where the instance (node) is launched.

PrivatePrimaryIp.description: The private primary IP address of the SageMaker HyperPod cluster node.

PrivatePrimaryIpv6.description: The private primary IPv6 address of the SageMaker HyperPod cluster node when configured with an Amazon VPC that supports IPv6 and includes subnets with IPv6 addressing enabled in either the cluster Amazon VPC configuration or the instance group Amazon VPC configuration.

PrivateDnsHostname.description: The private DNS hostname of the SageMaker HyperPod cluster node.

Placement.description: The placement details of the SageMaker HyperPod cluster node.

AvailabilityZone.description: The Availability Zone where the node in the SageMaker HyperPod cluster is launched.

AvailabilityZoneId.description: The unique identifier (ID) of the Availability Zone where the node in the SageMaker HyperPod cluster is launched.

CurrentImageId.description: The ID of the Amazon Machine Image (AMI) currently in use by the node.

DesiredImageId.description: The ID of the Amazon Machine Image (AMI) desired for the node.

KubernetesConfig.description: The Kubernetes configuration applied to this node, showing both the current and desired state of labels and taints. The cluster works to reconcile the actual state with the declared state.

CurrentLabels.description: The current labels applied to the cluster node.

DesiredLabels.description: The desired labels to be applied to the cluster node.

CurrentTaints.description: The current taints applied to the cluster node.

DesiredTaints.description: The desired taints to be applied to the cluster node.

CapacityType.description: The capacity type of the node. Valid values are OnDemand and Spot. When set to OnDemand, the node is launched as an On-Demand instance. When set to Spot, the node is launched as a Spot instance.

Status.description: Status of the cluster policy.

FailureReason.description: Failure reason of the cluster policy.

ClusterArn.description: ARN of the cluster where the cluster policy is applied.

SchedulerConfig.description: Cluster policy configuration. This policy is used for task prioritization and fair-share allocation. This helps prioritize critical workloads and distributes idle compute across entities.

CreationTime.description: Creation time of the cluster policy.

CreatedBy.description: Information about the user who created or modified a SageMaker resource.

LastModifiedTime.description: Last modified time of the cluster policy.

LastModifiedBy.description: Information about the user who created or modified a SageMaker resource.

PriorityClasses.description: List of the priority classes, PriorityClass, of the cluster policy. When specified, these class configurations define how tasks are queued.

FairShare.description:
When enabled, entities borrow idle compute based on their assigned FairShareWeight.

When disabled, entities borrow idle compute based on a first-come first-serve basis.

Default is Enabled.

CodeRepositoryArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Git repository.

CreationTime.description: The date and time that the repository was created.

LastModifiedTime.description: The date and time that the repository was last changed.

GitConfig.description: Configuration details about the repository, including the URL where the repository is located, the default branch, and the Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Amazon Web Services Secrets Manager secret that contains the credentials used to access the repository.

RepositoryUrl.description: The URL where the Git repository is located.

Branch.description: The default branch for the Git repository.

SecretArn.description:
The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Amazon Web Services Secrets Manager secret that contains the credentials used to access the git repository. The secret must have a staging label of AWSCURRENT and must be in the following format:

{"username": UserName, "password": Password}

CompilationJobName.description: The name of the model compilation job.

CompilationJobArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the model compilation job.

CompilationJobStatus.description: The status of the model compilation job.

CompilationStartTime.description:
The time when the model compilation job started the CompilationJob instances.

You are billed for the time between this timestamp and the timestamp in the CompilationEndTime field. In Amazon CloudWatch Logs, the start time might be later than this time. That's because it takes time to download the compilation job, which depends on the size of the compilation job container.

CompilationEndTime.description: The time when the model compilation job on a compilation job instance ended. For a successful or stopped job, this is when the job's model artifacts have finished uploading. For a failed job, this is when Amazon SageMaker AI detected that the job failed.

StoppingCondition.description: Specifies a limit to how long a model compilation job can run. When the job reaches the time limit, Amazon SageMaker AI ends the compilation job. Use this API to cap model training costs.

InferenceImage.description: The inference image to use when compiling a model. Specify an image only if the target device is a cloud instance.

ModelPackageVersionArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the versioned model package that was provided to SageMaker Neo when you initiated a compilation job.

CreationTime.description: The time that the model compilation job was created.

LastModifiedTime.description: The time that the status of the model compilation job was last modified.

FailureReason.description: If a model compilation job failed, the reason it failed.

ModelArtifacts.description: Information about the location in Amazon S3 that has been configured for storing the model artifacts used in the compilation job.

ModelDigests.description: Provides a BLAKE2 hash value that identifies the compiled model artifacts in Amazon S3.

RoleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of an IAM role that Amazon SageMaker AI assumes to perform the model compilation job.

InputConfig.description: Information about the location in Amazon S3 of the input model artifacts, the name and shape of the expected data inputs, and the framework in which the model was trained.

OutputConfig.description: Information about the output location for the compiled model and the target device that the model runs on.

VpcConfig.description: A VpcConfig object that specifies the VPC that you want your compilation job to connect to. Control access to your models by configuring the VPC. For more information, see Protect Compilation Jobs by Using an Amazon Virtual Private Cloud.

DerivedInformation.description: Information that SageMaker Neo automatically derived about the model.

MaxRuntimeInSeconds.description:
The maximum length of time, in seconds, that a training or compilation job can run before it is stopped.

For compilation jobs, if the job does not complete during this time, a TimeOut error is generated. We recommend starting with 900 seconds and increasing as necessary based on your model.

For all other jobs, if the job does not complete during this time, SageMaker ends the job. When RetryStrategy is specified in the job request, MaxRuntimeInSeconds specifies the maximum time for all of the attempts in total, not each individual attempt. The default value is 1 day. The maximum value is 28 days.

The maximum time that a TrainingJob can run in total, including any time spent publishing metrics or archiving and uploading models after it has been stopped, is 30 days.

MaxWaitTimeInSeconds.description:
The maximum length of time, in seconds, that a managed Spot training job has to complete. It is the amount of time spent waiting for Spot capacity plus the amount of time the job can run. It must be equal to or greater than MaxRuntimeInSeconds. If the job does not complete during this time, SageMaker ends the job.

When RetryStrategy is specified in the job request, MaxWaitTimeInSeconds specifies the maximum time for all of the attempts in total, not each individual attempt.

MaxPendingTimeInSeconds.description:
The maximum length of time, in seconds, that a training or compilation job can be pending before it is stopped.

When working with training jobs that use capacity from training plans, not all Pending job states count against the MaxPendingTimeInSeconds limit. The following scenarios do not increment the MaxPendingTimeInSeconds counter:

  • The plan is in a Scheduled state: Jobs queued (in Pending status) before a plan's start date (waiting for scheduled start time)

  • Between capacity reservations: Jobs temporarily back to Pending status between two capacity reservation periods

MaxPendingTimeInSeconds only increments when jobs are actively waiting for capacity in an Active plan.

Status.description: Status of the compute allocation definition.

FailureReason.description: Failure reason of the compute allocation definition.

ComputeQuotaConfig.description: Configuration of the compute allocation definition. This includes the resource sharing option, and the setting to preempt low priority tasks.

ComputeQuotaTarget.description: The target entity to allocate compute resources to.

ActivationState.description:
The state of the compute allocation being described. Use to enable or disable compute allocation.

Default is Enabled.

CreationTime.description: Creation time of the compute allocation configuration.

CreatedBy.description: Information about the user who created or modified a SageMaker resource.

LastModifiedTime.description: Last modified time of the compute allocation configuration.

LastModifiedBy.description: Information about the user who created or modified a SageMaker resource.

ResourceSharingConfig.description: Resource sharing configuration. This defines how an entity can lend and borrow idle compute with other entities within the cluster.

Strategy.description:
The strategy of how idle compute is shared within the cluster. The following are the options of strategies.

  • DontLend: entities do not lend idle compute.

  • Lend: entities can lend idle compute to entities that can borrow.

  • LendandBorrow: entities can lend idle compute and borrow idle compute from other entities.

Default is LendandBorrow.

BorrowLimit.description:
The limit on how much idle compute can be borrowed.The values can be 1 - 500 percent of idle compute that the team is allowed to borrow.

Default is 50.

PreemptTeamTasks.description:
Allows workloads from within an entity to preempt same-team workloads. When set to LowerPriority, the entity's lower priority tasks are preempted by their own higher priority tasks.

Default is LowerPriority.

If you don't provide a KMS key ID, Amazon SageMaker uses the default KMS key for Amazon S3 for your role's account.

PresetDeploymentType.description: The deployment type SageMaker Edge Manager will create. Currently only supports Amazon Web Services IoT Greengrass Version 2 components.

PresetDeploymentConfig.description:
The configuration used to create deployment artifacts. Specify configuration options with a JSON string. The available configuration options for each type are:

  • ComponentName (optional) - Name of the GreenGrass V2 component. If not specified, the default name generated consists of "SagemakerEdgeManager" and the name of your SageMaker Edge Manager packaging job.

  • ComponentDescription (optional) - Description of the component.

  • ComponentVersion (optional) - The version of the component.

    Amazon Web Services IoT Greengrass uses semantic versions for components. Semantic versions follow a major.minor.patch number system. For example, version 1.0.0 represents the first major release for a component. For more information, see the semantic version specification.

  • PlatformOS (optional) - The name of the operating system for the platform. Supported platforms include Windows and Linux.

  • PlatformArchitecture (optional) - The processor architecture for the platform.

    Supported architectures Windows include: Windows32_x86, Windows64_x64.

    Supported architectures for Linux include: Linux x86_64, Linux ARMV8.

HomeEfsFileSystemId.description: The ID of the Amazon Elastic File System managed by this Domain.

SingleSignOnManagedApplicationInstanceId.description: The IAM Identity Center managed application instance ID.

SingleSignOnApplicationArn.description: The ARN of the application managed by SageMaker AI in IAM Identity Center. This value is only returned for domains created after October 1, 2023.

Status.description: The status.

CreationTime.description: The creation time.

LastModifiedTime.description: The last modified time.

FailureReason.description: The failure reason.

SecurityGroupIdForDomainBoundary.description: The ID of the security group that authorizes traffic between the RSessionGateway apps and the RStudioServerPro app.

DefaultUserSettings.description: Settings which are applied to UserProfiles in this domain if settings are not explicitly specified in a given UserProfile.

AppNetworkAccessType.description:
Specifies the VPC used for non-EFS traffic. The default value is PublicInternetOnly.

  • PublicInternetOnly - Non-EFS traffic is through a VPC managed by Amazon SageMaker AI, which allows direct internet access

  • VpcOnly - All traffic is through the specified VPC and subnets

TagPropagation.description: Indicates whether custom tag propagation is supported for the domain.

DefaultSpaceSettings.description: The default settings for shared spaces that users create in the domain.

ModelSignature.description: The signature document of files in the model artifact.

If you don't provide a KMS key ID, Amazon SageMaker uses the default KMS key for Amazon S3 for your role's account.

PresetDeploymentType.description: The deployment type SageMaker Edge Manager will create. Currently only supports Amazon Web Services IoT Greengrass Version 2 components.

PresetDeploymentConfig.description:
The configuration used to create deployment artifacts. Specify configuration options with a JSON string. The available configuration options for each type are:

  • ComponentName (optional) - Name of the GreenGrass V2 component. If not specified, the default name generated consists of "SagemakerEdgeManager" and the name of your SageMaker Edge Manager packaging job.

  • ComponentDescription (optional) - Description of the component.

  • ComponentVersion (optional) - The version of the component.

    Amazon Web Services IoT Greengrass uses semantic versions for components. Semantic versions follow a major.minor.patch number system. For example, version 1.0.0 represents the first major release for a component. For more information, see the semantic version specification.

  • PlatformOS (optional) - The name of the operating system for the platform. Supported platforms include Windows and Linux.

  • PlatformArchitecture (optional) - The processor architecture for the platform.
